title: Chapter 306 – Hyeon Won
content:
"Here stands the true divine sword of the country!"

The weight of that phrase—"divine sword"—was immense, reverberating through both military and civil spheres alike. All the more so when spoken by a Deputy Commander of the first rank in praise of Radiant Demon Wing Leader Lightning Genius.

The crowd spread wide around them.

Many of the Shanxi aristocrats wore expressions twisted into something unreadable.

The Gongya Family estate was draped in desolate silence. Even the scrape of stone fragments rolling across the ruins could be heard with perfect clarity.

Slowly.

Dozens of local powerhouses assumed prostrating positions. Silk robes of every color brushed against walls, tiles, and the skeletal remains of pillars, producing soft whispers of fabric.

As each rose from their knees to stand upright, a dignified reverence poured from them—prayer-like, fit for royalty.

Martial aristocrats.

They had built their power through internal energy and technique. By deftly avoiding the scrutiny of officials, they had reigned as absolute rulers over the common people.

With no major sects established in Shanxi, they had easily risen to become martial families worthy of the title "aristocrat," seizing control of the military's swords and spears through every survival tactic at their disposal. Nothing stood in their way.

Woong.

A faint resonance of energy rippled between them.

Their internal energy had been woven together like threads, forming a network of multiple sound transmissions. From that point on, no conversation leaked outward.

"What is happening...?"

"He called him a divine sword!"

"So I didn't mishear after all."

"I don't understand. Why such favor? He tarnished the dignity of the Five Armies Command..."

"This is the worldly wisdom of a monster bred in Beijing politics. He has his own calculations. It must be advantageous not to antagonize Radiant Demon Wing Leader here."

"This goes beyond avoiding hostility—this borders on friendship!"

Zhuge Clan Leader and Radiant Demon Wing Leader.

Both were martial artists from foreign lands. Lightning Genius would depart soon, and Zhuge Liang had crossed the River Styx.

Meanwhile, the Shanxi martial artists gathered here would go on living in this land.

"I was considering teaching Divine Blood Extreme Demon a lesson... Would that displease the Deputy Commander?"

"Hard to say. Judging by his demeanor, his favor seems confined to Lightning Genius alone..."

"Quiet. Seeing how Radiant Demon Wing Leader cherishes his seniors so deeply, there's no telling what he might do with the Deputy Commander backing him."

"That's right. I heard he carries the Tyrant Hero's bloodline."

These were largely conversations among martial aristocrats who pursued fame above all else.

In a famine-stricken martial world, survival took precedence over the righteous path; spreading one's martial might mattered more than principle. Face was secondary.

They had planned to challenge the black-ranks from Desolate Fortress to duels, demonstrating their superior martial prowess.

The moment had been planned for when Lightning Genius beheaded Zhuge Clan Leader. The aim was to prevent foreign martial artists' names from disrupting the existing order.

By doing so, they intended to further solidify their claims to land, shops, and livestock farming.

"What a pity, what a pity."

Looking at the three black-ranks from Desolate Fortress who had survived the fierce battle, it was hard to say there was no opportunity.

Divine Blood Extreme Demon Jin Myeong-jo appeared to have been severely wounded even before the decisive battle. The blood that had poured from his abdomen while shielding the sphere containing Radiant Demon Wing Leader would have filled several gourds and then some.

Now he stood at a distance with arms folded, firm as a pillar, but the recovery of his wounds—etched in straight lines by the Yin and Yang Divine Whip—was noticeably slow.

Desolate Spear Yue Shou-lin, who was narrowing her eyes at the Deputy Commander, fared little better.

Having absorbed the concentrated brunt of Zhuge Clan Leader's restraint, she showed signs of serious internal injuries. She would likely struggle to operate even the true energy of her three meridians.

"We must give up."

"Let's simply placate the Gongya Family."

"I was planning to buy the farming rights in Pingyang Prefecture at a bargain, but that's empty talk now. Since the Gongya clan has connections with Radiant Demon Wing Leader..."

"Weren't we too harsh on the Gongya Family? Our disciples were going in and out of their storehouses as if they were their own bedrooms."

"If we apologize first, what can they do? It's not as though one Gongya clan can oppose every martial family in Shanxi."

The conversations continued with studied indifference. Their eyes never stopped assessing the appearance and condition of the three black-ranks.

Where else might they ever see Divine Blood Extreme Demon and Desolate Spear in such a battered state?

Lightning Genius, who had even suffered a penetrating wound to his shoulder, went without saying.

Everyone had confirmed his drastically weakened energy waves immediately after he beheaded Zhuge Clan Leader.

He must have experienced devastating internal energy depletion to kill a peerless master.

His sagging shoulders looked impossibly heavy, as if his entire body had been soaked in blood that still refused to stop flowing.

Stripped of his bearing and the authority of his black robe, he would have looked like nothing more than a boy of his age.

Prey for the wolves.

There was no need to actively antagonize him.

In an era where etiquette and law were losing their meaning, they could politely request a duel and perform just slightly below their true ability, and the aristocrats gathered here would praise one another.

As someone who had held their own against a black-rank's techniques.

Shanxi was their land. It would be even better if the Desolate Fortress side declined the duel—an opportunity to restore the face that Zhuge Clan Leader's manifestation had tarnished.

Fabricating and spreading rumors like "the black-rank was reluctant to engage with Shanxi martial families" would be no trouble at all.

"Let's watch for an opportunity. They'll need considerable time for recovery regardless."

A middle-aged swordsman who had casually draped a fur coat over his silk martial robe muttered the words. Like the other aristocrats, luxury radiated from every inch of him.

However, some aristocrats with more measured gazes did not join in the conversation that resembled a reckless wager. They offered only brief observations.

"The martial prowess Lightning Genius displayed—what he accomplished—is equivalent to cutting down the entire Zhuge family. It's genuinely shocking. I believe we should feel fear before anything else..."

"Indeed. Was Zhuge Clan Leader truly in an ordinary state?"

"From the outset, Desolate Spear and Divine Blood Extreme Demon are monsters capable of terrifying power even with just a handful of energy. If you imagine facing Desolate Spear Yue Shou-lin without the counter-methods Zhuge Clan Leader prepared... it sends chills down your spine."

Afterward, they kept their thoughts to themselves.

What would follow from all these events, whether the duel could end appropriately, whether they had failed to consider the troubles ahead...

And they realized.

This was the era where hunger and possessiveness robbed reason, the times when chivalry lost its fertile ground to raw martial might.

"Before I became Deputy Commander, I once met the Tyrant Hero... Lightning Genius, your face doesn't greatly resemble his. Your temperament—no, your spirit seems somewhat similar, though."

"What did you say?"

"Oh my, I seem to have misspoken. Don't look so serious."

"What do you mean by misspoken...?"

"......" The aristocrats quietly observed the Deputy Commander and Lightning Genius.

Standing on ground cracked like Shanxi's farmland, with the faint sound of Desolate Spear's giggling drifting from somewhere nearby.

***

An extremely grand feast was held.

It served as a preliminary to the martial arts tournament. The most storied family in Pingyang Prefecture—the Gongya Family—hosted the banquet.

The fragrance of delicacies and fine wines mingled above the massive U-shaped table.

"I didn't expect the Deputy Commander to step forward so publicly. I know he's been troubled by the movements of the Profound Martial Alliance's martial forces, so I wonder if collaboration between Desolate Fortress and the military might follow."

"I don't know how the situation in Shanxi will unfold from here. How many sects have decided to join under Transcendence City Sect..."

"Sister, try this. The braised beef is sliced paper-thin. It looks like a swordsman's handiwork."

"My disciple has pinpointed the assassins' location. Soon we'll have an opportunity to wipe them out..."

Dozens of conversations intertwined like tangled threads.

The main topics were accounts of the day Zhuge Clan Leader fell and speculations about what would follow.

The atmosphere bore no resemblance to the eve of the martial arts tournament.

The aristocrats conversed in hushed tones across the silk tablecloths, while the three black-ranks from Desolate Fortress spoke among themselves within a semi-transparent energy barrier.

All three had appeared despite their serious injuries.

It was a show of the squad leaders' resilience, and an effort to make amends for the damage inflicted on the Gongya Family. They couldn't simply complete their mission and leave after bringing such calamity upon the family that had aided Desolate Fortress.

They needed to prevent the accumulation of ill rumors that would cripple their subordinates' movements.

Meanwhile, a great many glances were cast at Radiant Demon Wing Leader.

He paid them no mind.

Jeong Yeon-shin held his cup with the practiced ease of someone familiar with the rituals of drinking, occasionally rolling it lightly in his palm.

He was in the midst of refining his martial arts through sword discussions with his seniors.

Every so often, Jin Myeong-jo would shoot an uncomfortable glance his way, one foot propped on the edge of the energy barrier.

"Your recovery could be delayed. Alcohol can wait until after."

Divine Blood Extreme Demon said it sternly.

Jeong Yeon-shin briefly closed his mouth and compared his height with his senior's.

"......"

Still a full head shorter.

Eventually, he silently placed his wine cup on the corner of the table.

His fingertips lingered against the rim, betraying his regret. With a short life ahead of him, such pleasures would be few.

"...Anyway, Senior Jin's foot technique has countless variations in its directional energy release. I wondered whether it might be possible to form a brief formation using instantaneous energy. There was much of that in the main branch's Thousand Gold Weightless Drum."

"Hmm."

"I believe it would be more beneficial to use the Eight Trigrams as an axis rather than the Six Harmonies. For someone like you, Senior..."

"You mean it's better to choose the one that allows more variations. With my energy sense, I should be able to control it adequately."

Jin Myeong-jo spoke while cold sweat traced down his back. Seizing the initiative was his specialty. The same held in conversation.

If you cut off words before they can branch and spread, it becomes far easier to maintain face.

A seasoned black-rank was prepared for any situation.

'As expected of Senior Jin.'

The corner of Jeong Yeon-shin's mouth rose slightly. It felt like conversing with a real person for the first time in a while.

"Senior, you are correct."

The brief rest granted after the fierce battle was deeply enjoyable. He felt certain that someday he would look back on this day with fondness.

"Today, just pour it in! Pour it all in! Huh?! Who did our Lightning Genius kill? None other than Zhuge Jian, who committed such a filial impiety that even the Jade Emperor would have been shocked and overturned! I'm saying he killed Zhuge Jian!"

A potent reek of alcohol rose from beneath his chin, accompanied by slurred speech.

This was the culprit who had compelled Jeong Yeon-shin—despite his pain—to erect an energy barrier before the sword discussion could begin.

Yue Shou-lin reached out with her small hand as if to swat his backside. Jeong Yeon-shin dodged with a practiced movement.

"His swordplay was absolutely artistic! Cut through both protective energy and dantian at once, huh? Kyaha...!"

"Grandma."

"She's trying to drown the pain in her three meridians through intoxication."

Jin Myeong-jo said.

"Zhuge Clan Leader's counter-method for the Yue Family Spear was extraordinarily vicious. Worthy of the Zhuge family, famous for their eye techniques. He must have studied the Shandong Yue family for a very long time."

"Then the reason Senior Yue's energy waves are unstable is...?"

"He focused more on severing true energy than on techniques. Since the Yin and Yang Divine Whip, as a mystical secret skill, can interfere with the very foundation of internal energy, the backlash must have been no ordinary thing. Think of it as torture equivalent to having your muscles torn from bone while she was protecting you."

"What do you mean, muscles torn from bone! It was nothing!"

Yue Shou-lin grinned and reached out her hand.

She was shorter than Jeong Yeon-shin. Raising her palm high, she seemed to be trying to pat the top of her junior's head.

Jeong Yeon-shin didn't dodge.

Finding this amusing, she settled for patting his back a few times instead.

"Well done, our youngest! You've become my strongest competitor now. Even Annihilation Team and Wish-fulfilling Spring Team will have to stay on their guard."

The three squad leaders of Desolate Fortress.

There was no gap to squeeze through between them. That's how it appeared from the outside.

Even the Gongya Family's eldest daughter and second son, let alone the Shanxi aristocrats, could only watch from a distance.

'It was a difficult mission.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought of the companions who would shield the squad leaders when exhaustion took hold.

The reason for a fortress's existence. In that moment, he simultaneously recalled the letter from an unidentified sender and Lazy Flame Dragon's letter.

[The ruffian of the Hwangbo family, the illegitimate child of Blazing Moon Great Slaughter Sect, and the treasured jade of Desolate Divine Sword are in my possession. Radiant Demon Wing Leader, come to Taiyuan Prefecture immediately upon seeing this letter and thrust your Northern Light Divine Sword into the largest tombstone at the burial mountain.]

[...A letter filled with all sorts of babbling will be coming your way, but please wait a little. If the squad leader steps forward again like in Shaanxi, the Radiant Demon Wing old fogies will descend to bury me.

Anyway, I've found a clue to unlock the internal energy restriction of Great Tongue Sword. Honestly, I didn't think this would actually exist.]

It was then.

Thud.

The Deputy Commander approached, his gray fur coat billowing beneath the brim of his pitch-black official hat. His cold eyes swept across the surrounding aristocrats.

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader, I have urgent matters to discuss with you."
